channel.title: American-Revolution on Wheeler Genealogy
channel.description: Recent content in American-Revolution on Wheeler Genealogy

item.title: The Two Solomon Wheelers: How the Warrant in Hand Slipped Away
item.description: One day of record work proved the 1777 Rockingham County warrant belongs to Solomon Wheeler of Kingston, not my ancestor Solomon Wheeler of Westmoreland. Here is how the two men are definitively separated.

item.title: The Warrant in Hand: Solomon Wheeler Arrests a Counterfeiter, April 1777
item.description: The Morgan Library document has arrived. Solomon Wheeler’s own handwriting — signed ‘Dep. Sher.’ — on a 1777 counterfeiting warrant signed by Josiah Bartlett and Meshech Weare is now in hand.

item.title: Finally A Wheeler
item.description: A long-running Wheeler question: did my 4th great-grandfather Solomon Wheeler support the American Revolution through civil service? New evidence points to civil law enforcement activity in 1777 New Hampshire that may connect to my Solomon.
